name: agentdb-semantic-vector-search
description: Build semantic vector search systems with AgentDB for intelligent document retrieval, RAG applications, and knowledge bases using embedding-based similarity matching
capabilities: Set up AgentDB vector database with embedding model configuration
Generate and store document embeddings with metadata
Build HNSW search index for optimized retrieval
Implement REST API query interface with filtering
Add hybrid search combining vector and keyword matching
Apply re-ranking to improve result relevance
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing markdown SOP files with TypeScript code examples. No executable code exists. All 31 static findings are false positives: (1) 'external_commands' patterns are TypeScript template literals and npm command examples, not Ruby backtick execution; (2) 'network' patterns are legitimate documentation URLs; (3) 'blocker' patterns (weak crypto, system reconnaissance) are technical terms in code comments (HNSW index parameters, SHA references). This skill provides guidance for implementing vector search and poses no security risk.
valueStatement: Users need intelligent document retrieval that understands meaning, not just keywords. This skill provides a 5-phase SOP to implement semantic vector search using AgentDB for RAG systems, knowledge bases, and context-aware querying.
userTitle: Build semantic vector search with AgentDB
limitations: Requires external embedding model API (OpenAI, Cohere, etc.)
AgentDB must be installed and configured separately
Does not include pre-built embedding generation code
Search accuracy depends on embedding model quality

useCases.title: Build RAG Systems
useCases.description: Create retrieval-augmented generation backends for LLMs with semantic search capability

useCases.title: Document Search API
useCases.description: Deploy semantic search endpoints for enterprise document retrieval systems

useCases.title: Knowledge Base Queries
useCases.description: Build intelligent knowledge bases with meaning-based document search and similarity matching

promptTemplates.title: Basic Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Use AgentDB to set up a semantic vector search system. Configure it with 1536 dimensions for OpenAI ada-002 embeddings. Show how to initialize the database and embedding model.

promptTemplates.title: Document Indexing
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement document embedding and storage. Process a corpus of documents, generate embeddings for each, and store them in AgentDB with metadata including title, content, and category.

promptTemplates.title: Search API
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a REST API endpoint for semantic search. Accept query text, generate embedding, search with topK parameter, apply metadata filters, and return ranked results.

promptTemplates.title: Hybrid Search
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement hybrid search combining vector similarity with keyword matching. Add re-ranking to improve relevance scores. Configure alpha parameter for vector and keyword balance.

bestPractices: Use appropriate embedding dimensions for your model (1536 for OpenAI ada-002, 768 for sentence-transformers)
Apply metadata filtering to reduce search space and improve relevance for specific document types
Monitor query latency and adjust HNSW parameters (M, efConstruction) based on accuracy requirements
antiPatterns: Storing documents without metadata limits filtering and re-ranking capabilities
Using mismatched embedding dimensions causes indexing and search failures
Ignoring re-ranking reduces result quality for complex queries

faq.question: Which embedding models are compatible?
faq.answer: AgentDB supports OpenAI ada-002, sentence-transformers, Cohere, and any model producing fixed-dimensional vectors.

faq.question: What is the maximum document count?
faq.answer: AgentDB handles millions of vectors with HNSW indexing. Performance depends on available memory.

faq.question: Can this integrate with existing databases?
faq.answer: AgentDB runs alongside PostgreSQL, MongoDB, or any database. Use it as the vector layer only.

faq.question: Is my data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: AgentDB stores vectors locally. Embedding API calls go to your chosen provider with your credentials.

faq.question: Why are my search results poor?
faq.answer: Check embedding model quality, try re-ranking, adjust alpha for hybrid search, or increase topK for broader results.

faq.question: How does this compare to Pinecone or Weaviate?
faq.answer: AgentDB offers self-hosted deployment, no API fees, and tight integration with Claude Code workflows.
